name: Always-on AI meeting assistants & 'consent-by-bot'
summary: Notetaker bots and built-in meeting AI now join calls and transcribe by default. A bot announcing itself, or a meeting platform's 'recording' banner, is increasingly treated as the consent mechanism — but whether that satisfies all-party consent (especially for participants who never affirmatively agreed) is unsettled.

name: CIPA § 631 session-replay & chatbot wiretap class actions
summary: A large wave of California class actions argues that website session-replay scripts and third-party chatbot vendors 'wiretap' visitors in real time without consent under the California Invasion of Privacy Act. Courts are split on motions to dismiss, and the theory keeps extending to new technologies.

name: Body cameras & the right to record
summary: Body-worn cameras are now standard for police, and courts broadly recognize a First Amendment right to openly record officers in public. This sits in tension with all-party consent statutes, several of which have been held unconstitutional as applied to recording public officials.
